allies.rationale_text: Circle-of-control sharpens resilience by keeping effort on factors within reach instead of spreading attention across uncontrollable stressors.
allies.source_quote: Limiting concerns and efforts only to factors within one's control.

allies.rationale_text: Scientific-method-evidence-testing helps resilience learn from stress by treating actions as hypotheses to test rather than as identity commitments.
allies.source_quote: By framing actions as hypotheses to be tested and actively seeking to confirm or disprove them, resilience is built into the strategic process.

allies.rationale_text: Root-cause-analysis strengthens resilience by moving recovery from symptom management toward redesign of the stress-producing system.
allies.source_quote: the entire machine can undergo an evolutionary process—continually encountering problems, diagnosing them, designing changes, and implementing them

antagonists.rationale_text: Confirmation-bias blocks resilience because adaptation requires learning from stress signals rather than defending the current recovery story.
antagonists.source_quote: Confirmation Bias prevents the objective analysis necessary for resilience by causing individuals to seek information that confirms pre-existing beliefs.

antagonists.rationale_text: Status-quo-bias fights resilience when recovery is defined as returning to the old state instead of reconfiguring to fit the changed environment.
antagonists.source_quote: recovery from disruption defaults to restoring pre-crisis conditions rather than pursuing the strategic reconfiguration that the changed environment demands

structured_tensions.tension_text: Resilience conflicts with growth-mindset when persistence under stress is mistaken for learning progression without capability update.
structured_tensions.source_quote: resilience vs growth-mindset: resilience conflicts with growth-mindset (growth mindset) when persistence under stress is mistaken for learning progression without capability update.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Resilience conflicts with adaptation when recovery routines stabilize the current system but block necessary strategic change.
structured_tensions.source_quote: resilience vs adaptation: resilience conflicts with adaptation (adaptation) when recovery routines stabilize the current system but block necessary strategic change.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Resilience conflicts with confirmation-bias when stress tests are designed to confirm existing recovery plans rather than expose genuine failure modes.
structured_tensions.source_quote: resilience vs confirmation-bias: resilience conflicts with confirmation-bias (confirmation bias) when stress tests are designed to confirm existing recovery plans rather than expose genuine failure modes that the plan cannot handle.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Resilience conflicts with status-quo-bias when recovery from disruption defaults to restoring pre-crisis conditions rather than strategic reconfiguration.
structured_tensions.source_quote: resilience vs status-quo-bias: resilience conflicts with status-quo-bias (status quo bias) when recovery from disruption defaults to restoring pre-crisis conditions rather than pursuing the strategic reconfiguration that the changed environment demands.

curation_notes.summary: The raw source is strong on adaptive allies plus an explicit tension block around false recovery and bias-protected persistence.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor motivational relation sprawl beyond the source-backed control, testing, and anti-bias surface.
open_questions: Growth-mindset is source-real as both support and tension surface, but the explicit tension block was clearer than trying to preserve a symmetric ally relation.
